message: beads: expand completion epic + proof binder + diversity gate

 {"id":"bd-5fw.1","title":"Closure contract schema v1: objective release invariants by replacement level","description":"Background:
- We need an explicit, machine-checkable definition of "finished" for L0/L1/L2/L3 so release claims are objective.

Scope:
- Define a canonical closure contract schema with versioning.
- Encode required invariants per level: symbol support claims, call-through limits, test gates, perf ceilings, artifact proofs.

Deliverables:
1) `closure_contract.v1` schema (human+machine readable).
2) Level-specific obligation matrix with pass/fail predicates.
3) Mapping from each obligation to a concrete command/artifact path.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Contract can be validated automatically.
- No requirement remains ambiguous or manual-only.
- Any unmet invariant yields deterministic failure reason.

Rationale:
- This creates a stable control plane for all downstream beads and prevents claim drift.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for schema validation and invariant rule parsing.
- E2E dry-run over intentionally broken contracts.
- Structured logs: trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445449915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445431891Z","close_reason":"Implemented closure_contract.v1 schema + deterministic validator gate + broken-contract e2e tests + structured log enforcement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","governance","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.1. Plan: (1) define closure_contract.v1 schema and level obligations in docs, (2) implement machine validator + deterministic diagnostics, (3) add unit tests and broken-contract tests, (4) wire structured release-check logs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:53:10Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered closure contract schema + validator gate + integration tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/closure_contract_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include closure contract gate)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test closure_contract_test -- --nocapture (6 passed)\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh (PASS)\n\nContract supports machine-validated level obligations (L0-L3), transition invariant mapping, deterministic failure reasons, and structured JSONL logs with required fields (trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.2","title":"Deterministic gate orchestration DAG with fail-fast + resume semantics","description":"Background:
- A closure contract is only useful if there is one deterministic execution order for all gates.

Scope:
- Define and implement canonical gate order: lint -> unit -> conformance -> e2e -> perf -> docs/reports -> release dossier.
- Implement fail-fast semantics with reproducible retries and explicit resume points.

Deliverables:
1) Gate DAG with required inputs/outputs per step.
2) Runner orchestration spec and implementation notes.
3) Operator runbook for partial reruns without invalidating evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same inputs always produce same gate order and same result.
- Resume behavior is deterministic and auditable.

Rationale:
- Prevents ad-hoc gate sequencing and accidental green builds.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for DAG validity and resume logic.
- E2E tests for fail-fast and resume flows.
- Logs: trace_id, gate_name, prereq_hash, status, duration_ms, resume_token.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.355931474Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.337574584Z","close_reason":"Implemented release gate DAG artifact + deterministic fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + integration tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["orchestration","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.671238102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.2 immediately after closing bd-5fw.1. Plan: define deterministic release gate DAG artifact + implement fail-fast/resume orchestrator script + add harness tests for ordering, fail-fast, and resume-token determinism.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:04Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered deterministic release gate DAG + fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_dag.v1.json\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_runner_runbook.md\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/release_gate_dag_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include release dry-run orchestration)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_gate_dag_test -- --nocapture (5 passed)\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh --mode dry-run (PASS)\n\nFail-fast/resume semantics validated with intentional e2e gate failure and deterministic resume token replay (resume_skip for prior gates; restart at failed gate index).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.3","title":"Release artifact dossier spec + integrity validator","description":"Background:
- Final readiness depends on evidence completeness, not just exit codes.

Scope:
- Specify required artifact dossier: conformance outputs, replacement profile, support/reality reports, perf baselines, logs index, decision explainability bundle.
- Build a validator that enforces completeness and integrity (checksums + schema validation).

Deliverables:
1) Artifact dossier manifest format.
2) Integrity validator and missing-artifact diagnostics.
3) Compatibility policy for dossier format evolution.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Missing or malformed artifact blocks release deterministically.
- Dossier can be revalidated independently after generation.

Rationale:
- Ensures future audits can reconstruct exactly why a release was accepted.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for manifest parser and checksum verification.
- E2E tests for success, missing file, and corrupt artifact scenarios.
- Logs: trace_id, artifact_id, schema_version, digest, validation_result.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:47:11.699751037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","diagnostics","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.448365477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-29b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:06:13.384091700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-30o.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.522937289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.238203688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ot.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.632641217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.770712980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:47:11.699675606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.338694730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-5fw.4","title":"Proof Obligations Binder: theorem -> evidence -> gate mapping","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:37.571322581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:37.571322581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","proof","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:37.571322581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-5fw.4","title":"Proof Obligations Binder: theorem -> evidence -> gate mapping","description":"Background:
- We already have many gates (closure contract, evidence compliance, runtime_math governance, perf budgets, replacement guards).
- What is missing is a single self-contained binder that answers, mechanically: "what do we claim?" -> "what artifact proves it?" -> "what gate enforces it?".
- Without this binder, release readiness devolves into manual interpretation and doc drift.

Goal:
- Define a versioned proof-obligations binder (operational theorems -> evidence artifacts -> gates) and integrate it into the release dossier so release acceptance is deterministic.

Deliverables:
1) Proof binder schema (v1): obligation_id, statement, quantified_scope (APIs/families/modes/input-domain/environment), evidence_artifacts (paths + sha256), gates (scripts/*), and join keys (trace_id/mode/gate/api_family|symbol/bead_id).
2) Canonical mapping table for each obligation to concrete artifacts and enforcing gates.
3) Dossier integration: extend the Release Artifact Dossier spec (`bd-5fw.3`) so every release candidate includes the proof binder + referenced artifacts.
4) Deterministic validator + failure-injection tests (missing obligation artifact -> deterministic violation_code + remediation_hint).

Proof Obligations Checklist (release-critical, from project plan):
1. Strict Refinement Theorem (strict mode matches reference glibc on defined inputs).
2. Hardened Safety Theorem (hardened mode preserves safe-state invariants or denies with defined error behavior).
3. Deterministic Replay Theorem (same (mode, inputs, env snapshot) -> identical decisions + evidence).
4. Non-Regression Sequential Guarantee (anytime-valid monitors maintain bounded false alarm rates under continuous testing).
5. CPOMDP Safety Feasibility Guarantee (repair controller never emits inadmissible action).
6. Superoptimization Soundness Guarantee (every accepted hot-kernel rewrite has stored equivalence certificate + isomorphism proof).
7. Barrier Invariance Theorem (safe set forward-invariant under admitted actions).
8. Robustness Radius Theorem (policy stays within safety/latency budgets inside declared ambiguity set).
9. Concurrent Linearizability Theorem (metadata ops linearizable under stated memory model assumptions).
10. HJI Viability Theorem (runtime remains in computed viability kernel under adversary model).
11. Sheaf Global-Consistency Theorem (declared inconsistency classes detected by overlap/cohomology diagnostics).
12. Interaction-Coverage Lower-Bound Guarantee (campaign achieves declared t-wise coverage).
13. Coupled-Divergence Bound (strict vs hardened divergence bounded on declared domains).
14. Mean-Field Equilibrium Stability Theorem (controller parameters converge under workload classes).
15. Schrodinger Morphing Entropy Bound (regime transitions bounded in transport cost/overshoot).
16. SOS Certificate Soundness Theorem (synthesized polynomial certs imply invariants).
17. Large-Deviation Risk Bound (catastrophic event probabilities below thresholds).
18. Topological Shift Detectability Theorem (telemetry topology shifts detectable per declared classes).
19. Rough-Signature Stability Bound (trace features stable under bounded perturbations).
20. Tropical Latency Composition Bound (end-to-end worst-case bound preserved under composition).
21. Primal-Dual Convergence Guarantee (online constrained updates converge or safely roll back).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Release dry-run produces a dossier containing the proof binder and the binder validates in a clean checkout.
- Missing/invalid binder evidence fails deterministically and points to the exact missing artifact + gate.
- Binder is contributor-readable: obligations are operationally stated and mapped to concrete commands/scripts.

Test Plan:
- Unit: binder schema parsing/validation.
- Integration: failure-injection (missing artifact, wrong checksum, missing join keys).
- E2E: release dry-run over a small fixture corpus, verifying binder + dossier integrity.

Constraints:
- Tooling only: binder/validators live in harness/scripts/CI, not in libc runtime.
- Deterministic by construction: stable ordering of obligations and stable violation codes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:37.571322581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:58.856225621Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","proof","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:37.571322581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:58.856176419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-5fw.5","title":"Branch-Diversity Gate: enforce multi-family math obligations per milestone","description":"Background:
- AGENTS.md and PLAN_TO_PORT_GLIBC_TO_RUST.md impose a hard branch-diversity rule: each major milestone must use at least 3 distinct math families, and must include at least one obligation from (conformal statistics, algebraic topology, abstract algebra, Grothendieck-Serre methods). SIMD/ABI milestones additionally require Atiyah-Singer/K-theory/localization and Clifford obligations.
- Today we have math governance classification (production vs research) but no mechanical enforcement that milestone obligation sets satisfy diversity constraints.

Goal:
- Define and enforce a deterministic branch-diversity gate so milestones cannot ship with mathematical monoculture or missing required categories.

Deliverables:
1) Milestone taxonomy: define what counts as a "major milestone" in this repo (at minimum: L0/L1/L2/L3 replacement level claims; core subsystems like allocator/threading/stdio/loader; and runtime-math admission milestones).
2) Obligation tagging schema: for each milestone, enumerate required obligations and tag each with math-family labels.
3) Deterministic gate script (tooling-only): fail CI when a milestone obligation set violates diversity constraints; output actionable diagnostics (missing families; family dominance >40%; missing required categories).
4) Unit tests for the gate on synthetic milestone specs + one integration test against the repo’s declared milestone specs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Gate is deterministic, low-noise, and blocks release-dossier generation when constraints are violated.
- Clear remediation guidance is emitted (which obligations to add/retire).

Test Plan:
- Unit: schema validation + dominance calculation + required-category checks.
- Failure-injection: intentionally omit categories and verify deterministic error codes.

Constraints:
- Developer transparency: this gate must not require contributors to understand the math; it operates on tags and declared obligations.
- Tooling-only: no runtime libc dependency changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:43.358869332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:43.358869332Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","governance","math","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:43.358869332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5ky","title":"Harness: Regression report for strict vs hardened (latency/risk Pareto)","description":"Create a report that summarizes tradeoffs.

Contents:
- Strict vs hardened deltas in latency (p50/p95) and risk/repair rates.
- Pareto cumulative regret trends.
- Any FDR/alpha-investing and approachability signals.

Goal:
- Make it obvious when a change improves safety but violates latency budget, or vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.328425057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192491089Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192472995Z","close_reason":"Added strict-vs-hardened runtime_math regression report (per-mode subprocess metrics + Markdown/JSON output)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:49.994980835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:49.915354009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66s","title":"Stub wave 2: getaddrinfo/getnameinfo numeric+files backend","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement numeric host/service resolution and /etc/hosts + /etc/services files backend.
- Deterministic error mapping for unsupported address families/protocols.

Acceptance:
- netdb fixtures pass for numeric and files-backed paths.
- Support matrix marks partial scope explicitly (DNS network backend tracked separately).

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.921326511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","resolver","stubs"],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-66s","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Core resolver module: crates/glibc-rs-core/src/resolv/mod.rs
- AddrInfo struct, EAI_* error codes, AF_* constants
- /etc/hosts parsing: parse_hosts_line, lookup_hosts, reverse_lookup_hosts
- /etc/services parsing: parse_services_line, lookup_service
- getaddrinfo(): numeric IPv4/IPv6 resolution, wildcard fallback
- getnameinfo(): sockaddr_in/sockaddr_in6 to string conversion
- eq_ignore_ascii_case helper
- 35 unit tests covering all functions

### 2. ABI layer: crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs (pre-existing, 519 lines)
- getaddrinfo: numeric IPv4/IPv6 parsing, port parsing, membrane validation
- freeaddrinfo: linked list cleanup
- getnameinfo: numeric address-to-string conversion
- gai_strerror: full EAI_* error mapping

### 3. Status updated: support_matrix.json
- getaddrinfo, getnameinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror: Stub -> Implemented
- Total: Implemented 87->91, Stub 6->2

### 4. Cascading spec updates
- packaging_spec.json: symbol_distribution, Stub_remaining, blockers
- replacement_levels.json: assessment counts, stub_breakdown
- stub_priority_ranking.json: removed resolv_abi from module/symbol rankings, updated burn_down

### Test commands
```
cargo test -p glibc-rs-core --lib resolv  # 35 pass
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness            # 213+ pass, 0 failures
```

All quality gates pass: clippy clean, fmt clean, full harness green.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-66wz","title":"bd-25n subtask: Allocator+membrane invariant/property/adversarial test expansion","description":"Background:
- Allocator/membrane safety claims require broad invariant and adversarial coverage.

Goal:
- Expand allocator/membrane test packs to cover temporal safety, bounds, cache invalidation, and healing behavior comprehensively.

Deliverables:
1) Unit tests for arena generation/quarantine edge cases.
2) Property tests for pointer validation monotonicity and state transitions.
3) Adversarial tests for double-free, foreign-free, UAF, overflow markers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- High-risk allocator/membrane invariants are directly tested and documented.
- Failures report invariant name and violating trace context.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit/property suites with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs with invariant_id, case_id, mode, outcome, timing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.887565117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.887565117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","critique","testing","unit","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-6yd","title":"Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure","description":"Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script. Artifact filenames, bounds, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist all validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.930667114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Crash bundle specification operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_crash_bundle.sh validates evidence snapshots, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","diagnostics"]}
 {"id":"bd-73r","title":"EPIC: Runtime Hot-Path Perf Gates (strict <20ns, hardened <200ns)","description":"Goal: enforce extreme-software-optimization discipline for runtime_math.

Why:
- runtime_math is on the membrane hot path. Any regression here leaks into every libc call we gate.

Constraints:
- Strict mode must stay <20ns/call overhead target.
- Hardened mode must stay <200ns/call overhead target.
- No behavior regressions without explicit isomorphism proof + fixture evidence.

Outputs:
- Microbench + profile baselines for RuntimeMathKernel::decide + observe_validation_result.
- Golden snapshot outputs to detect accidental semantic drift.
- A repeatable baseline/profile/verify loop that future work MUST follow.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.483973301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:28:13.676103762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:28:04.198742106Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-1om","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.963092567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.182677202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-38w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.033841207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.105370048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.822099661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.892070636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:07.149122119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-73r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Extreme-Optimization Discipline For runtime_math

This epic encodes the non-negotiable loop: **baseline -> profile -> prove -> implement -> verify -> repeat**.

### What We Are Protecting
runtime_math runs at the ABI boundary of libc call paths. Any accidental overhead shows up everywhere.

Budgets (from AGENTS.md):
- strict overhead target: <20ns/call (fast exits)
- hardened overhead target: <200ns/call (bounded heavy path)

### What Counts As “Hot Path”
- `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` on the strict path
- per-call evidence recording (if enabled)
- pointer-validation stage ordering / cached biases

### Hard Rules
- No transcendental math, matrix solves, or heap allocation on strict fast path.
- Any stochastic element must be deterministic from seed (replayable).
- Any “expensive” kernel must be cadence-driven (epoch update) not per call.

### Measurement Artifacts Required
- Criterion microbench for `decide` / `observe` (ns/op + percentiles)
- Profile recipe (flamegraph/perf) pinned to a reproducible command
- Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate
- Isomorphism proof template for policy changes (behavior invariants)

### Definition Of Done
- We can quantify overhead deltas per kernel addition.
- We can detect regressions in CI before merging.
- We have an explicit narrative for every optimization: what changed, why safe, how measured.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:00Z"},{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-73r","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"EPIC CLOSED by CobaltCompass.  {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}
 {"id":"bd-7cba","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Minimal codec scope/exclusion ledger + compatibility intent","description":"Background:
- iconv scope must be explicit to avoid overpromising full iconvdata parity before deterministic generation pipeline exists.

- Define minimal codec scope, exclusions, and compatibility intent for phase progression.

2) Compatibility goals per included codec family.
3) Support matrix mapping for supported/unsupported codecs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scope is explicit and user-facing claims are accurate.
- No ambiguous codec support status.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for scope manifest parsing/validation.
- Structured logs for scope checks and drift detections.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","locale","spec","verification"]}

 {"id":"bd-7ef9","title":"bd-h5x subtask: Callthrough census + decommission sequencing by symbol family","description":"Background:
- Callthrough elimination work needs symbol-family-level truth to avoid hidden dependence on host glibc.

Goal:
- Produce current callthrough census and decommission plan by family/symbol with risk and sequence.

Deliverables:
1) Enumerate all remaining callthrough symbols and their owning modules.
2) Rank by runtime impact and replacement complexity.
3) Define migration order with rollback strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is machine-readable and tied to support matrix.
- Decommission plan is dependency-aware and implementation-ready.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for census extraction/parsing helpers.
- Structured logs for census generation and ranking decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.598635951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.598635951Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callthrough","critique","docs","syscall","verification"]}
